label: behaviour change intervention scenario
definition: A planned process in which a BCI is applied in a given context, including BCI engagement and outcome behaviour.

label: behaviour change intervention outcome behaviour
definition: Human behaviour that is an intervention outcome.

label: behaviour change intervention
definition: An intervention that has the aim of influencing human behaviour.

label: behaviour change intervention context
definition: An aggregate of entities that are not dependent on the intervention but may influence the effect of a BCI on its outcome behaviour.

label: behaviour change intervention mechanism of action
definition: A process that is causally active in the relationship between a BCI scenario and its outcome behaviour.

label: social environmental system
definition: An <environmental system> that consists of people, their pets, social and cultural institutions and processes that influence the life of people and social groups.

label: family environmental system
definition: A <social environmental system> that consists of persons related as members of a domestic group, or a number of domestic groups linked through descent from a common ancestor, marriage,  adoption or other legal arrangements.

label: friendship network
definition: A <social environmental system> that consists of persons who share a bond of mutual affection and are in contact or communication.

label: mental skill
definition: A <mental capability> acquired through training or practice.

label: self-regulation capability
definition: A <mental capability> that involves processes that modulate the frequency, rate or extent of a response to external or internal stimuli and that are instigated by the person themselves.

label: behavioural self-regulation capability
definition: A <self-regulation capability> to modulate one's behaviour.

label: cognitive self-regulation capability
definition: A <self-regulation capability> to modulate one's cognitive processes.

label: emotional self-regulation capability
definition: A <self-regulation capability> to modulate one's emotional responses to a stimulus.

label: physical behavioural capability
definition: A <behavioural capability> that involves sensory or musculoskeletal abilities.

label: physical skill
definition: A <physical behavioural capability> acquired through training or practice.

label: social behavioural capability
definition: A <personal capability> includes interpersonal behaviour in its realisation.

label: social skill
definition: A <social behavioural capability> acquired through training or practice.

label: social alienation
definition: A <mental disposition> to perceive or experience oneself as isolated from and not meaningfully involved in social groups.

label: awareness
definition: A <mental disposition> that is realized by experiencing events, objects, sensory patterns or cognitive representations.

label: behavioural intention
definition: A <mental disposition> to commit to enact or not enact a behaviour.

label: belief about barriers to a behaviour
definition: A <belief> about a factor that could restrict or impede a person from engaging in a behaviour.

label: belief about conformity to behavioural norms
definition: A <belief> about the extent to which one's own behaviour is similar to that of referent others.

label: belief about consequences of behaviour
definition: A <belief about consequences of an occurrence> in terms of what results from or follows the performance of a behaviour.

label: belief about social consequences of behaviour
definition: A <belief about consequences of behaviour> in terms of their impact on the social environmental system.

label: belief about consequences of goal attainment
definition: A <belief about consequences of an occurrence> in terms of the outcomes resulting from achieving a goal.

label: belief about control over one's future
definition: A <belief> about the extent to which one can direct what happens in one's future.

label: belief about gain
definition: A <belief> about the existence of an increase from an initial reference point.

label: belief about likelihood of consequences of behaviour
definition: A <belief about likelihood of consequences of an occurrence> in terms of the probability that a behaviour will result or not result in particular outcomes.

label: belief about likelihood of health consequences of behaviour
definition: A <belief about likelihood of consequences of behaviour> in terms of the probability of a behaviour resulting or not resulting in a change to health or wellbeing.

label: belief about likelihood of consequences of an occurrence
definition: A <belief about consequences of an occurrence> in terms the probability that a given event or state will occur or not occur in the future.

label: belief about reduction
definition: A <belief> about the existence of a decrease from an initial reference point.

label: belief about message relevance
definition: A <belief about message> in terms of whether the content of a verbal, graphic, written or recorded communication is relevant to oneself.

label: belief about ones environment
definition: A <belief> about the nature of the immediate physical and social settings in which people live, including  culture and the people and institutions with whom they interact.

label: belief about severity of an outcome
definition: A <belief> about how serious the harm associated with an outcome could be.

label: belief about personal susceptibility
definition: A <belief about susceptibility to a threat> in terms of one's personal vulnerability to a threat.

label: belief about the timing of the consequences of behaviour
definition: A <belief about consequences of behaviour> in terms of how long after the performance of the behaviour they will occur.

label: belief about choice over a behaviour
definition: A <belief> about the extent to which one is free to choose whether to perform the behaviour.

label: evaluation of self
definition: An <evaluative belief> about one's attributes.

label: evaluative belief
definition: A <belief> about whether a particular aspect of the world is positive or negative.

label: perceived norm
definition: A <belief about one's social environment> in terms of what is typical for people who belong to a particular group.

label: perceived descriptive behavioural norm
definition: A <perceived norm> regarding the prevalence of performance of a given behaviour by people within a group.

label: perceived evaluative behavioural norm
definition: A <perceived norm> regarding whether a behaviour is appropriate and correct for people who belong to a particular group.

label: normative belief
definition: A <perceived norm> regarding whether key others think one should perform a behaviour.

label: self-efficacy belief for a behaviour and its associated outcomes
definition: A <self-efficacy belief> to organise and execute a behaviour and achieve the outcomes associated with this behaviour.

label: situational self-efficacy belief for a behaviour
definition: A <self-efficacy belief for a behaviour> when a barrier or facilitator of the behaviour is encountered.

label: cognitive schema
definition: A <mental disposition> that when activated, guides an interconnected network of perception, thought, emotion or behaviour.

label: action schema
definition: A <cognitive schema> that when activated, guides a sequence of behaviours.

label: decision
definition: A <mental disposition> to represent one proposition as preferred from at least two.

label: behavioural decision
definition: A <decision> that one behaviour is preferred from at least two behavioural options.

label: goal
definition: A <cognitive representation> of an end state towards which one is striving.

label: knowledge
definition: A <mental disposition> to understand the nature of the world, or a specific aspect of the world, that corresponds to the actual state of the world and is acquired through experience or learning.

label: knowledge regarding a behaviour
definition: <Knowledge> regarding a particular set of actions.

label: knowledge regarding an innovation
definition: <Knowledge> regarding a novel invention or way of doing something.

label: knowledge regarding a threat
definition: <Knowledge> regarding a potential harm.

label: knowledge regarding treatment
definition: <Knowledge> regarding a therapy or medication for a health problem.

label: learned stimulus-reponse co-occurrence
definition: A <mental disposition> to think or behave in a particular way in response to an internal or external event in the person's environment, which is acquired through associative learning.

label: mental imagery disposition
definition: A <mental disposition> to evoke the representation of the sensory characteristics of objects or events when these are not immediately present to the senses.

label: motivation to comply
definition: A <behavioural motivation> to act in accordance with the likely approval of others.

label: motivational orientation
definition: A <motivational disposition> to be guided by a type of outcome.

label: prevention focused motivational orientation
definition: A <motivational orientation> in which motivation is guided by a focus on the presence or absence of negative outcomes.

label: promotion focused motivational orientation
definition: A <motivational orientation> in which motivation is guided by a focus on the presence or absence of positive outcomes.

label: personal value
definition: A <mental disposition> to regard certain things as fundamentally important in life, which informs standards for behaviour.

label: psychological need
definition: A <mental disposition> of a person to act to obtain or maintain a particular state due to this state’s importance to the person’s wellbeing.

label: need for autonomy
definition: A <psychological need> to have a sense of personal agency and initiative.

label: need for self-esteem
definition: A <psychological need> to have a positive evaluation of oneself and one's qualities and characteristics.

label: need for a sense of safety
definition: A <psychological need> to feel safe from threats.

label: need for a sense of belonging and attachment
definition: A <psychological need> to have caring relationships with other people.

label: need to feel competent
definition: A <psychological need> to experience oneself as capable and effective.

label: mental plan for a behaviour
definition: A <mental plan> that is realised in mental processes representing the steps to enact a behaviour and the conditions under which the behaviour will be enacted.

label: stereotype
definition: A <cognitive schema> that when activated, guides perception, thought, emotion or behaviour toward members of a social group as having certain shared attributes, by virtue of their group membership.

label: social embeddedness
definition: A <mental disposition> to experience a feeling of being connected by social attachments.

label: appraisal of obligation to act
definition: An <emotional-relevance appraisal> that represents an evaluation of how much one is personally obliged to respond to an event or person in need.

label: desired standard
definition: A <cognitive representation> of a reference level that an individual wishes to obtain.

label: mental image
definition: A <cognitive representation> of the sensory characteristics of objects or events that are not immediately present to the senses.

label: personal role
definition: A <role> that inheres in a human being by virtue of their social and institutional circumstances.

label: social role
definition: A <personal role> that is realised in human social processes.

label: location
definition: A spatial <quality> that inheres in a bearer by virtue of its position relative to other entities.

label: behavioural opportunity
definition: An <environmental disposition> that is required for or facilitates a behaviour.

label: financial behavioural opportunity
definition: A <physical behavioural opportunity> that involves the person having sufficient financial resources.

label: healthcare access
definition: A <behavioural opportunity> regarding how easy it is for a person to approach and use a healthcare service.

label: physical behavioural opportunity
definition: A <behavioural opportunity> that involves time and parts of the environmental system that do not involve people or organisations.

label: social behavioural opportunity
definition: A <behavioural opportunity> that involves the social environmental system.

label: temporal behavioural opportunity
definition: A <physical behavioural opportunity> that involves the person having sufficient time or a suitable period.

label: internal stimulus for behaviour
definition: An <internal stimulus> that gives rise to a behaviour.

label: mental stimulus for behaviour
definition: A <internal stimulus for behaviour> that arises from mental processes.

label: normative behaviour
definition: A <socially-related behaviour> that is commonly enacted by people that are part of a social environmental system.

label: goal pursuit process
definition: A <bodily process> in which attempts are made to achieve a desired end state.

label: social influence behaviour
definition: An <inter-personal behaviour> where a person exerts an influence on the behaviour of another.

label: internal reward for a response
definition: A <bodily process> by which the person experiences an internally-generated positive physical or psychological state subsequent to a response.

label: plan enactment
definition: A <bodily process> by which a person attempts to follow the steps in a plan.

label: self-regulation of behaviour
definition: A <self-regulation process> that modulates the frequency, rate or extent of one's  performance of a behaviour.

label: behavioural self-regulation of behaviour
definition: <Self-regulation of behaviour> through the performance of a different behaviour.

label: introjected self-regulation
definition: <Self-regulation of behaviour> undertaken to manage a sense of guilt, anxiety or obligation.

label: self-reinforcing self-regulation
definition: <Self-regulation of behaviour> through self-administering a reward for performing an intended behaviour.

label: value-congruent self-regulation
definition: <Self-regulation of behaviour> undertaken to achieve consistency between one's values and the behaviour performed.

label: cognitive dissonance
definition: A <subjective affective feeling> that is unpleasant and experienced due to perceived inconsistency between two concurrently held cognitive representations.

label: feeling an urge
definition: A <subjective affective feeling> that  arises when an impulse to enact a behaviour is activated but not immediately acted upon.

label: comprehension
definition: A <cognitive process> that creates a representation of the meaning and significance of a communication, object, event or situation.

label: decision simplification
definition: A <cognitive process> used prior to decision-making that reduces the perceived scope or complexity of available options.

label: reflective thinking
definition: <Thinking> that involves the generation of inferences by reasoning based on one or more statements, evaluating anticipated consequences or considering the relationships between concepts.

label: dissonance reduction process
definition: A <bodily process> through which a perceived inconsistency between two concurrently held cognitive representations is reduced.

label: goal setting process
definition: A <mental process> that establishes a cognitive representation of the desired end state.

label: heuristic process
definition: A <mental process> that uses simple rules and associations learnt from experience to make judgements.

label: decision-making
definition: <Judging> in which one or more propositions or behaviours are identified as preferred from a larger number.

label: social comparison process
definition: <Judging> oneself or one's social group in relation to the qualities or characteristics of another person or social group.

label: associative learning
definition: <Learning> that involves changing the strength of association between two or more mental processes.

label: classical conditioning
definition: <Associative learning> that occurs when the pairing of a conditioned and unconditioned stimulus leads to the conditioned stimulus producing a response that would otherwise have only been produced by the unconditioned stimulus.

label: discrimination learning
definition: <Associative learning> in which people learn to distinguish between appropriate responses to similar but different stimuli depending on whether a generalised response is rewarded or not.

label: extinction in classical conditioning
definition: <Associative learning> in which the strength of a conditioned response is reduced because its conditioned stimulus is presented without being accompanied by the unconditioned stimulus.

label: extinction in operant learning
definition: <Associative learning> in which the rate of behavioural response in the presence of a discriminative stimulus is reduced because the response is no longer followed by a reinforcer.

label: stimulus generalisation in learning
definition: <Generalisation in learning> concerning the antecedent stimulus.

label: operant conditioning
definition: <Associative learning> in which the likelihood of a particular behavioural response is influenced by the past consequences associated with performing that behaviour.

label: associative memory
definition: A <memory process> that deals with the receipt, storage, retrieval and modification of information regarding a set of entities associated with a particular object or event.

label: episodic memory
definition: A <memory process> that deals with the receipt, storage, retrieval and modification of information regarding a personally experienced event at a particular time or place.

label: semantic memory
definition: A <memory process> that deals with the receipt, storage, retrieval and modification of information associated with meanings, understandings or conceptual facts about the world.

label: procedural memory
definition: A <memory process> that deals with the receipt, storage, retrieval and modification of information regarding how to perform a complex activity without conscious awareness.

label: iconic memory
definition: A <memory process> that deals with the receipt, storage, retrieval and modification of information regarding a brief visual stimulus.

label: mental categorising
definition: A <mental process> in which objects, events, people, or experiences are grouped into classes, on the basis of features shared by members of the same class and features distinguishing the members of one class from those of another.

label: mentally comparing against a standard
definition: A <mental process> in which conditions are compared against a particular reference level.

label: behavioural motivation
definition: A <mental process> that energises and directs a behaviour.

label: automatic behavioural motivation
definition: <Behavioural motivation> that arises from emotions and impulses that result from associative learning or innate dispositions.

label: intrinsic motivation
definition: <Autonomous motivation> due to a behaviour's inherent enjoyment, satisfaction or benefits to oneself.

label: self-monitoring
definition: A <monitoring process> in which one observes one's own behaviour or mental processes.

label: belief about anticipated emotion
definition: A <belief about consequences of an occurrence> in terms of the potential emotions experienced as result of an event.

label: belief about message
definition: A <belief> regarding aspects of a verbal, graphic, written or recorded communication.

label: belief about the credibility of a message's source
definition: A <belief about message> in terms of whether a verbal, graphic, written or recorded communication is provided by people or institutions who provide reliable information.

label: belief about message trustworthiness
definition: A <belief about message> in terms of whether a verbal, graphic, written or recording communication provides information that can be relied on as honest or truthful.

label: belief about need satisfaction
definition: A <belief> regarding whether one or more of the person's needs have been met.

label: belief about ones social environment
definition: A <belief about one's environment> in terms of the aspects of one’s immediate social settings in which people live, including culture and the people and institutions with whom they interact.

label: belief about social support
definition: A <belief about one's social environment> in terms of whether others will provide or are available to provide assistance or comfort to help the person cope with one or more stressors or enhance their wellbeing.

label: evaluative belief about a behaviour
definition: An <evaluative belief> about whether a behaviour is positive or negative.

label: evaluative belief about the consequences of behaviour
definition: A <belief about consequences of behaviour> in terms of whether they are positive or negative.

label: belief about the personal desirability of consequences of a behaviour
definition: An <evaluative belief about the consequences of behaviour> in terms of the value of the consequences of behaviour to oneself.

label: evaluative belief about others
definition: An <evaluative belief> about the attributes of others.

label: belief about control over behaviour
definition: A <belief> about one's capabilities and opportunities within an environmental system to perform a behaviour or achieve the outcomes resulting from a behaviour.

label: self-efficacy belief for a behaviour
definition: A <self-efficacy belief> to organise and execute a behaviour.

label: learned stimulus-behaviour co-occurrence
definition: A <learned stimulus-response co-occurrence> that involves a behavioural disposition in response to an internal or external event in the person's environment, which is acquired through habit learning.

label: learned stimulus-thought co-occurrence
definition: A <learned stimulus-response co-occurrence> that involves a disposition to think in a particular way in response to an internal or external event in the person's environment, which is acquired through associative learning.

label: mental plan for coping with barriers
definition: A <mental plan for a behaviour> regarding a strategy to deal with conditions that could prevent the performance of a behaviour.

label: habitual behaviour
definition: An <individual human behaviour> that results from a learnt stimulus-behaviour co-occurrence.

label: pleasure associated with behaviour
definition: <Pleasure> that is experienced as a result of engaging in a behaviour.

label: avoidance mental process
definition: A <mental process> that reduces the frequency by which an aversive cognitive representation is evoked.

label: mental distancing
definition: A <cognitive process> that involves intentionally detaching oneself from one's immediate thoughts.

label: targeted mental distancing
definition: <Mental distancing> that involves identifying particular immediate thoughts and intentionally detaching from these thoughts.

label: approval-based motivation
definition: A <need-based motivation> related to gaining or maintaining social approval or a positive self-evaluation.

label: belief about others' susceptibility
definition: A <belief about susceptibility to a threat> in terms of how vulnerable others are to a threat.

label: autonomous motivation
definition: <Behavioural motivation> that is internally generated and based on a behaviour being interesting or valuable to an individual.

label: belief about responsibility to act
definition: A <belief about responsibility> to take a particular action.

label: behavioural importance-based motivation
definition: <Autonomous motivation> due to the perceived value or importance of the behaviour to oneself.

label: belief about susceptibility to a threat
definition: A <belief> about vulnerability to a threat.

label: belief about threat
definition: A <belief> about a potential harm.

label: extrinsic motivation
definition: <Behaviour motivation> that is based on external rewards, punishments or pressure regarding a behaviour.

label: need-based motivation
definition: <Autonomous motivation> due to a behaviour being perceived to satisfy a psychological need.

label: habit learning
definition: <Associative learning> that involves strengthening an association between a stimulus and an impulse through repetition of co-occurrence of the stimulus and the behaviour.

label: behaviour change intervention content
definition: An intervention content that is part of a behaviour change intervention.

label: goal directed BCT
definition: A <behaviour change technique> that sets or changes goals.

label: goal setting BCT
definition: A <goal directed BCT> that sets goals.

label: set behaviour goal BCT
definition: A <goal setting BCT> that sets a goal for the behaviour to be achieved.

label: agree behaviour goal BCT
definition: A <goal setting BCT> that involves the intervention source agreeing with the person on a behavioural goal.

label: set outcome goal BCT
definition: A <goal setting BCT> in which the goal is a positive outcome of performing the behaviour.

label: agree outcome goal BCT
definition: A <goal setting BCT> that involves the intervention source agreeing with the person on a goal which is a positive outcome of performing the behaviour.

label: goal strategising BCT
definition: A <goal directed BCT> in which the person analyses factors influencing the behaviour and generates, selects, or reviews strategies to increase facilitators and overcome barriers.

label: action planning BCT
definition: A <goal directed BCT> that involves making a detailed plan for the performance of the behaviour, which must include at least one of context, frequency, duration or intensity.

label: review behaviour goal BCT
definition: A <goal directed BCT> that reviews a behavioural goal and considers modifying the goal in light of progress toward the goal.

label: attend to discrepancy between current behaviour and goal BCT
definition: A <goal directed BCT> that draws attention to discrepancies between a person's current behaviour and the person's outcome goal, behavioural goal or action plan.

label: review outcome goal BCT
definition: A <goal directed BCT> that reviews an outcome goal and considers modifying the goal in light of achievement.

label: create behavioural contract BCT
definition: A <goal directed BCT> that creates a written specification of the behaviour to be performed, agreed on by the person, and witnessed by another person.

label: affirm commitment BCT
definition: A <goal directed BCT> that asks the person to affirm or reaffirm statements indicating commitment to change the behaviour.

label: advise goal integration BCT
definition: A <goal directed BCT> that advises the person to adopt more than one goal in a situation where the goals facilitate each other.

label: monitoring BCT
definition: A <behaviour change technique> that involves gathering or using information about performance.

label: observe behaviour without feedback BCT
definition: A <monitoring BCT> that observes current performance of the behaviour with the person’s knowledge but without providing feedback about their behaviour.

label: record behaviour without feedback BCT
definition: A <monitoring BCT> that records current performance of the behaviour with the person’s knowledge but without providing feedback about their behaviour.

label: observe outcome of behaviour without feedback BCT
definition: A <monitoring BCT> that observes an outcome of performing the behaviour with the person’s knowledge but without providing feedback about the outcome.

label: record outcome of behaviour without feedback BCT
definition: A <monitoring BCT> that records an outcome of performing the behaviour with the person's knowledge but without providing feedback about the outcome.

label: provide feedback BCT
definition: A <monitoring BCT> in which feedback about the behaviour is provided.

label: provide feedback on behaviour BCT
definition: A <provide feedback BCT> that provides information about the person's previous performance of the behaviour.

label: self-monitor behaviour BCT
definition: A <monitoring BCT> in which the person uses a method to monitor and record their behaviour.

label: self-monitor outcome of behaviour BCT
definition: A <monitoring BCT> in which the person uses a method to monitor and record an outcome of their behaviour.

label: provide biofeedback BCT
definition: A <provide feedback BCT> that provides information about the functioning or state of the person's body, based on information collected by an external monitoring device.

label: provide feedback on outcome of behaviour BCT
definition: A <provide feedback BCT> that provides information about an outcome of the person's previous performance of the behaviour.

label: social support BCT
definition: A <behaviour change technique> that involves taking steps to secure or deliver the support or aid of another person.

label: advise to seek support BCT
definition: A <social support BCT> that involves advising the person to seek support from another person.

label: advise to seek instrumental support BCT
definition: An <advise to seek support BCT> that suggests the person try to obtain support from another in terms of tangible aid.

label: advise to seek emotional support BCT
definition: An <advise to seek support BCT> that suggests the person try to obtain support from another in terms of expressing concern, caring and empathy.

label: advise to seek informational support BCT
definition: An <advise to seek support BCT> that suggests the person try to obtain support from another in terms of advice, suggestions and information.

label: advise to seek appraisal support BCT
definition: An <advise to seek support BCT> that suggests the person try to obtain support from another in terms of information that will aid the person in evaluating themself or the situation.

label: arrange support BCT
definition: A <social support BCT> that organises support from another for the person.

label: arrange instrumental support BCT
definition: An <arrange support BCT> that organises support from another in terms of tangible aid.

label: arrange emotional support BCT
definition: An <arrange support BCT> that organises support from another in terms of expressing concern, caring and empathy.

label: arrange informational support BCT
definition: An <arrange support BCT> that organises support from another in terms of advice, suggestions and information.

label: arrange appraisal support BCT
definition: An <arrange support BCT> that organises support from another in terms of information that will aid the person in evaluating themself or the situation.

label: deliver support BCT
definition: A <social support BCT> that directly provides support to the person.

label: deliver instrumental support BCT
definition: A <deliver support BCT> that provides tangible aid.

label: deliver emotional support BCT
definition: A <deliver support BCT> that provides expressions of concern, caring and empathy.

label: deliver informational support BCT
definition: A <deliver support BCT> that provides advice, suggestions and information.

label: deliver appraisal support BCT
definition: A <deliver support BCT> that provides information that will aid the person in evaluating themself or the situation.

label: guide how to perform behaviour BCT
definition: A <behaviour change technique> that provides guidance regarding how to perform the behaviour.

label: agree on how to perform behaviour BCT
definition: A <guide how to perform behaviour BCT> that involves reaching consensus on how to perform the behaviour.

label: inform about antecedents BCT
definition: A <suggest different perspective on behaviour BCT> that involves providing factual information to the person regarding triggers or influences that precede the initiation of the behaviour.

label: re-attribute cause BCT
definition: A <suggest different perspective on behaviour BCT> that involves eliciting the person's beliefs about, and suggesting alternative beliefs about, the causes of the behaviour.

label: conduct a behavioural experiment BCT
definition: A <behaviour change technique> that advises on how to identify and test hypotheses about the behaviour, its causes and consequences.

label: demonstrate the behaviour BCT
definition: A <guide how to perform behaviour BCT> that provides an observable sample of the performance of the behaviour for the person to aspire to or imitate.

label: reframe past behaviour BCT
definition: A <suggest different perspective on behaviour BCT> that involves reattributing a person's successes to internal, stable or global factors or failures to external, unstable or specific factors.

label: draw attention to incompatible beliefs BCT
definition: A <suggest different perspective on behaviour BCT> that draws the person's attention to the discrepancies between current or past behaviour and self-identity.

label: instruct how to perform a behaviour BCT
definition: A <guide how to perform behaviour BCT> that involves telling the person how to perform the behaviour.

label: remind about personal capability BCT
definition: A <prompt thinking related to successful performance BCT> that reminds the person of their skills and abilities that are relevant to the performance of the behaviour.

label: plan inclusion of enjoyment BCT
definition: A <goal directed BCT> that advises the person to plan a way of performing the behaviour that is pleasurable or satisfying.

label: increase awareness of consequences BCT
definition: A <behaviour change technique> that draws attention to consequences of the behaviour in the normal course of events.

label: inform about health consequences BCT
definition: An <increase awareness of consequences BCT> that provides information about the physical or mental health consequences of performing or not performing the behaviour.

label: inform about social consequences BCT
definition: An <increase awareness of consequences BCT> that provides information about the social consequences of performing or not performing the behaviour.

label: inform about emotional consequences BCT
definition: An <increase awareness of consequences BCT> that provides information about the emotional consequences of performing or not performing the behaviour.

label: monitor emotional consequences BCT
definition: A <monitoring BCT> that involves the person assessing their emotions after performing the behaviour.

label: induce anticipated regret BCT
definition: An <inform about emotional consequences BCT> that focuses on expectations of remorse after performing or not performing the behaviour.

label: increase salience of consequences BCT
definition: An <awareness of consequences BCT> that changes behaviour by the source emphasising the consequences of performing a behaviour to the person in a way that makes them memorable.

label: consider pros and cons BCT
definition: An <increase awareness of consequences BCT> that advises identification and comparison of the positive and negative consequences of performing or not performing the behaviour.

label: prompt comparative imagining of future outcomes BCT
definition: An <increase awareness of consequences BCT> that guides the person to imagine and compare the consequences of performing and not performing the behaviour.

label: awareness of other peoples thoughts, feelings and actions BCT
definition: A <behaviour change technique> that increases awareness of what other people think, do, or feel.

label: prompt social comparison BCT
definition: An <awareness of other people's thoughts, feelings and actions BCT> that draws attention to other people's behaviour and compares it with the person's own behaviour.

label: increase awareness of others approval BCT
definition: An <awareness of other people's thoughts, feelings and actions BCT> that increases awareness of whether others will like, approve, dislike, or disapprove of the behaviour.

label: present information from credible influence BCT
definition: An <awareness of other people's thoughts, feelings and actions BCT> that presents information from a credible person or organisation to influence the behaviour.

label: suggest to change behaviour BCT
definition: An <awareness of other people's thoughts, feelings and actions BCT> that involves sharing a view about what behaviour the person should do.

label: tell to change behaviour BCT
definition: An <awareness of other people's thoughts, feelings and actions BCT> that involves telling the person to perform the behaviour.

label: implore to change behaviour BCT
definition: An <awareness of other people's thoughts, feelings and actions BCT> that makes an emotional or earnest appeal to change the person's behaviour.

label: alter external stimulus BCT
definition: An <associative learning BCT> that involves creating, strengthening or reducing an association between the behaviour and an environmental trigger.

label: prompt intended action BCT
definition: An <alter external stimulus BCT> that involves introducing an external stimulus to facilitate the behaviour for which an intention has previously been formed.

label: cue BCT
definition: An <alter external stimulus BCT> that introduces external information that is already associated with the behaviour in order to elicit that behaviour.

label: cue signalling reward BCT
definition: An <alter external stimulus BCT> that identifies an external stimulus that reliably signals that reward will follow the behaviour.

label: reduce cue frequency BCTs
definition: An <alter external stimulus BCT> in which cues for the behaviour are presented less frequently.

label: expose to stimulus BCT
definition: An <alter external stimulus BCT> that sustains the person's exposure to a stimulus associated with the behaviour.

label: arrange satiation BCT
definition: An <expose to stimulus BCT> that provides sustained exposure to a stimulus to reduce or extinguish an unwanted behaviour.

label: systematically desensitise BCT
definition: An <expose to stimulus BCT> that gradually exposes the person to an aversive stimulus while also engaging them in a relaxation process to reduce the response to that stimulus.

label: associative learning BCT
definition: A <behaviour change technique> that involves repeated pairing of a stimulus with another stimulus or with a behavioural outcome.

label: associative learning to elicit behaviour BCT
