label: behaviour change intervention comparison evaluation study
definition: A BCI evaluation study that involves comparison between two or more BCI scenarios to produce one or more BCI effect estimates.

label: behaviour change intervention effect estimate
definition: A behaviour change intervention evaluation finding that characterises the difference between BCI outcome estimates of two BCI scenarios.

label: behaviour change intervention evaluation study
definition: An intervention evaluation study of a BCI scenario.

label: behaviour change intervention evaluation study risk of bias or error
definition: An information content entity that is about the likelihood of the BCI evaluation finding misrepresenting the outcome behaviour.

label: behaviour change intervention evaluation report
definition: A report that is a description of a BCI evaluation study.

label: behaviour change intervention dose
definition: A BCI content attribute that is its amount.

label: behaviour change intervention evaluation finding
definition: An evaluation finding that is the output of a BCI evaluation study.

label: behaviour change intervention evaluation study plan
definition: A plan for a BCI evaluation study.

label: behaviour change intervention outcome estimate
definition: A BCI evaluation finding that is about an outcome behaviour.

label: behaviour change intervention physical setting
definition: A physical environment in which a BCI is delivered.

label: country of intervention
definition: A geographical location of a country where the intervention takes place.

label: within-country location
definition: A geographical location within a country.

label: attribute of location
definition: Features of a given location, such as social and economic characteristics.

label: area social and economic condition
definition: An attribute of location describing the overall socioeconomic state of a location.

label: low-income area
definition: An area social and economic condition described to have a low-income, whether at a country or within-country level.

label: high-income area
definition: An area social and economic condition described to have a high-income, whether at a country or within-country level.

label: population and resource density
definition: An attribute of location describing the density of an area, in terms of people and resources within it.

label: suburban area
definition: A population and resource density that involves being on the edge of a large town or city where a proportion of those who work in the town or city live.

label: household residence
definition: A facility where an individual is living alone or with one or more person. The individuals do not have to be related.

label: multiple occupancy residence
definition: A facility where an individual lives with many others that may be collected according to a social structure such as education or disability.

label: student residence
definition: A multiple occupancy residence where many students live.

label: residential care or assisted living
definition: A multiple occupancy residence where multiple vulnerable people live

label: homeless setting
definition: A residential facility where an individual is living that is not stable and secure.

label: temporary residence
definition: A residential facility where individuals are in a transitional state of housing and not staying for a prolonged period.

label: hospital outpatient clinic facility
definition: A hospital facility to treat patients without them staying overnight, often after a hospital visit.

label: doctor-led primary care facility
definition: A healthcare facility led by doctors.

label: care home facility
definition: A healthcare facility that is run by a care home organization and is the bearer of a care home function.

label: drug or alcohol rehabilitation facility
definition: A rehabilitation facility to assist the recovery of people with drug or alcohol addiction.

label: community healthcare facility
definition: A clinic providing healthcare services to people in a certain area

label: community outpatient clinic facility
definition: A healthcare facility to treat patients in the community without them staying overnight.

label: dentist facility
definition: A healthcare facility where dental healthcare is provided.

label: educational facility
definition: A facility in which formal education is provided to a student population.

label: early years facility
definition: An educational facility in which pre-school education is provided.

label: primary school
definition: A school facility for younger children, typically aged between five and eleven.

label: middle school
definition: A school facility providing education between primary and secondary school.

label: secondary school
definition: A school facility for older children and teenagers, typically aged between eleven and eighteen.

label: vocational facility
definition: An educational facility providing practically based, occupationally-specific teaching.

label: university facility
definition: An educational facility in which students study for degrees and academic research is done.

label: community facility
definition: A facility used by a group of people living in the same place or having a particular characteristic in common.

label: sport and exercise facility
definition: A community facility used for exercising.

label: social centre or Community Hall facility
definition: A community facility used for socialising by those living in a given area.

label: library facility
definition: A community facility containing a collection of books and learning resources for loan.

label: religious facility
definition: A community facility where individuals or a group of people come to perform acts of devotion and veneration.

label: hospitality and catering facility
definition: A community facility used to serve food.

label: arts and entertainment facility
definition: A community facility designed to entertain or amuse.

label: retail facility
definition: A facility used as an outlet for shopping.

label: office facility
definition: A facility of a room, set of rooms, or building used as a place for commercial, professional, or bureaucratic work.

label: criminal justice facility
definition: A facility where individuals are being reprimanded, detained or imprisoned.

label: factory facility
definition: A facility of a building or group of buildings where goods are manufactured or assembled chiefly by machine

label: military facility
definition: A facility relating to or characteristic of soldiers or armed forces

label: transportation
definition: Methods of traveling from one place to another.

label: public transportation
definition: Forms of transportation that run on fixed routes and are available to the public, usually for a set fare

label: private transportation
definition: A form of transportation owned by an individual for individual or group use.

label: mobile intervention venue
definition: A form of transportation delivering interventions in transient locations.

label: ambulance
definition: A form of transporation which can transport patients for health treatment, and in some instances will also provide out-of-hospital healthcare to the patient.

label: outdoor environment
definition: A site which is an outdoor location outside of a building.

label: water
definition: An outdoor environment set in an expanse of water.

label: path or pavement
definition: An outdoor environment for the passage of persons or cyclists on land

label: path or pavement for pedestrians
definition: A path or pavement for the passage of persons only on land.

label: path or pavement for cyclists
definition: A path or pavement for the passage of people using bicycles only on land.

label: psychiatric facility
definition: A place designed and staffed to house and treat individuals that need assistance with mental dysfunctions

label: behaviour change intervention scenario report
definition: A report that describes a BCI scenario.

label: behaviour change intervention scenario plan
definition: A plan that is realized in a BCI scenario process.

label: behaviour change intervention social setting
definition: An aggregate of people with whom a BCI population interacts.

label: behaviour change intervention study investigator
definition: A role played by a person that contributes substantively to production or reporting of a BCI evaluation study.

label: behaviour change intervention study sample
definition: A research study sample whose behaviour is studied in a BCI evaluation study.

label: behaviour change intervention tailoring
definition: A BCI attribute in which the content or delivery of a BCI for some member of the BCI population varies according to their characteristics or setting.

label: behaviour change technique
definition: A <planned process> that is the smallest part of BCI content that is observable, replicable and on its own has the potential to bring about behaviour change.

label: population behaviour
definition: An aggregate of individual human behaviours of members of a population.

label: evaluation finding
definition: A data item that is the output of an intervention evaluation study.

label: individual human behaviour
definition: A <bodily process> of a human that involves co-ordinated contraction of striated muscles controlled by the brain.

label: human life function
definition: A function that inheres in a human being and is realised in processes that enable the human to survive and thrive.

label: non-linguistic communication behaviour using gesture
definition: A non-linguistic communication behaviour using body language involving a deliberately chosen movement of the head, neck, torso, or limbs to express an idea or feeling.

label: object management behaviour
definition: An environmental system management behaviour that involves creating, maintaining, adapting or destroying non-sentient objects.

label: environmental system management behaviour
definition: A material-entity related behaviour that involves creating, maintaining, adapting or destroying aspects of the physical or social environment system.

label: learning behaviour
definition: An experience-related behaviour that involves improving knowledge or skill.

label: social organisation behaviour
definition: A socially-related behaviour that involves a person contributing to the functioning of a social structure or a person in relation to a social structure.

label: self-injury behaviour
definition: A harmful behaviour that involves intentionally causing oneself physical harm.

label: creative expressive behaviour
definition: An expressive behaviour that involves consciously using some capability to create or shape an aspect of the environment to express an idea or emotion.

label: personal bodily care behaviour
definition: An individual human behaviour that attends to one’s own hygiene, comfort or appearance.

label: inter-personal behaviour
definition: An <socially-related behaviour> that involves an interaction between two or more people.

label: locomotive behaviour
definition: A position-related behaviour in which muscles are used by a person to move themselves relative to the immediate environment or part of it.

label: object-using behaviour
definition: A material-entity related behaviour that uses a non-living object.

label: physical impact behaviour
definition: A physical contact behaviour that is forceful.

label: posture behaviour
definition: A position-related behaviour that involves adopting a body configuration in relation to the immediate environment.

label: sexual behaviour
definition: An experience-related behaviour that involves sexual arousal.

label: bullying behaviour
definition: A harassment behaviour that involves targeting someone through deliberate misuse of power in a relationship to inflict physical, social or psychological harm.

label: care-giving behaviour
definition: A social support behaviour that involves meeting another person’s physical, psychological or social needs that this person is unable to fully meet themselves at a given moment.

label: human communication behaviour
definition: An <expressive behaviour> that involves the transmission of information between two or more people.

label: economic behaviour
definition: A socially-related behaviour that involves the production, acquisition, distribution or exchange of money, goods or services.

label: physical combat behaviour
definition: An inter-personal behaviour that involves taking part in a physical struggle involving contact or the use of weapons

label: harassment behaviour
definition: Harmful behaviour to others that entails intentionally targeting someone with a behaviour that has the effect of causing distress in the targeted person.

label: skill development behaviour
definition: A learning behaviour that involves improving the ability to perform physical, psychological or social activities.

label: mind-body behaviour
definition: An experience-related behaviour that aims to create a sense of interconnectedness between the mind and the body.

label: physical performance behaviour
definition: A health-related behaviour that involves maintenance or improvement of flexibility, strength, balance or cardiovascular fitness.

label: enjoyment behaviour
definition: An experience-related behaviour that is performed to experience pleasure or satisfaction.

label: excretion behaviour
definition: A life function-related behaviour that involves eliminating excess or harmful chemicals produced by bodily functions.

label: reproductive behaviour
definition: A life function-related behaviour that involves producing offspring based on combining DNA of two or more people.

label: breathing behaviour
definition: A life function-related behaviour involves providing an appropriate level of oxygenation to body tissues.

label: travel behaviour
definition: A position-related behaviour that involves changing physical location.

label: consumption behaviour
definition: A material entity-related behaviour that involves ingesting material into the body.

label: manufacturing behaviour
definition: An object-using behaviour that involves making a product from raw materials or parts.

label: reading behaviour
definition: An object-using behaviour that involves deriving meaning from written or printed symbols.

label: transporting behaviour
definition: A travel behaviour that involves moving an object in addition toother than the actor.

label: pro-social behaviour
definition: A socially-related behaviour that a population judges to accord with current norms of positive social conduct.

label: lying down
definition: A posture behaviour that involves being in a horizontal position on a supporting surface

label: reclining
definition: A posture behaviour in which one's weight is supported by one's buttocks and back rather than one's feet, and in which one's back rests on a surface at an angle that is greater than 90 degrees and less than 180 degrees.

label: sitting
definition: A posture behaviour in which one's weight is supported by one's buttocks rather than one's feet, and in which one's back is upright.

label: standing
definition: A posture behaviour in which one has or is maintaining an upright position while supported by one's feet

label: antisocial behaviour
definition: A socially-related behaviour that a population judges to be is contrary to the laws or accepted current norms of social conduct within a specific social context and causes annoyance and or disapproval in others.

label: avoidance behaviour
definition: An experience-related behaviour that involves taking defensive action in order to avoid some stimulus judged to be aversive by the person.

label: harmful behaviour
definition: An individual human behaviour that causes net harm.

label: impulsiveness
definition: A behavioural attribute that is to what extent the behaviour is a direct emotional, habitual or instinctive reaction to something.

label: association behaviour
definition: A social organisation behaviour that involves increasing a sense of being part of a social group or dyad.

label: nurture behaviour
definition: A socially-related behaviour that involves meeting the physical, psychological or social needs of another living being to promote its development.

label: political behaviour
definition: A socially-related behaviour that aims to bring about or oppose political or social change.

label: individual human behaviour pattern
definition: A uniform process aggregate whose member parts are behaviours of the same type and in the same person.

label: tobacco use behaviour pattern
definition: A substance use behaviour pattern that involves tobacco use.

label: tobacco smoking behaviour
definition: A tobacco use behaviour that involves setting light to a combustible tobacco product and ingesting the tobacco smoke that is produced.

label: tobacco smoking behaviour pattern
definition: A tobacco use behaviour pattern that involves tobacco smoking behaviour.

label: cigarette smoking behaviour
definition: A tobacco smoking behaviour that involves setting light to one end of a cigarette, putting the other end to the lips and sucking in order to ingest cigarette smoke.

label: cigarette smoking behaviour pattern
definition: A tobacco smoking behaviour pattern that involves cigarette smoking behaviour.

label: process aggregate
definition: An occurrent consisting exactly of a plurality of processes that are process_aggregate_member_parts_of that occurrent for all times at which it exists.

label: walking
definition: A locomotive behaviour that involves moving at a regular pace by lifting and setting down each foot in turn, never having both feet off the ground at once.

label: employment behaviour
definition: An economic behaviour that involves a paid work agreement between an employer and an employee.

label: spiritual behaviour
definition: An experience-related behaviour that is performed in line with a belief system that is grounded in reverence for a supernatural power or powers and provides meaning in life.

label: substance use behaviour
definition: A consumption behaviour that involves ingesting a psychoactive substance into the body.

label: tobacco use behaviour
definition: A substance use behaviour that involves ingesting constituents of a tobacco-containing product.

label: intervention
definition: A planned process that has the aim of influencing an outcome.

label: intervention evaluation study
definition: A research study that aims to assess attributes of an intervention with regards to their positive or negative value.

label: intervention outcome
definition: An entity that is influenced by an intervention

label: individual human activity
definition: A process that is produced by a person.

label: human population
definition: An object aggregate that consists of two or more people.

label: human behaviour
definition: A process that is an individual human behaviour or a population behaviour.

label: process attribute
definition: A process profile that is an attribute of a process.

label: behaviour change intervention style of delivery
definition: A communication style that is an attribute of a BCI content communication process.

label: intervention content communication process
definition: A communication that transmits the content of an intervention

label: behaviour change intervention content communication process
definition: An intervention content communication process that is about behaviour change intervention content

label: communication process attribute
definition: An attribute of a communication process.

label: communication style
definition: A particular manner of communicating aimed at inducing or avoiding certain kinds of responses in others, or demonstrating certain characteristics of the initiator.

label: intervention style of delivery
definition: A communication style that is an attribute of an intervention content communication process.

label: person-centred intervention delivery
definition: An intervention delivery characterised by efforts by an intervention source to give a person the resources they need to manage their own life and make them an active participant in deciding how to move forward.

label: participant-led intervention delivery
definition: An intervention delivery in which the participant takes the lead in determining the focus of the intervention.

label: source-led intervention delivery
definition: An intervention delivery in which the intervention person source takes the lead in determining the focus of the intervention.

label: active listening
definition: A communication involving one participant paying close attention to what is said by another, asking questions and reflecting as needed, in an attempt to fully understand what has been said.

label: affirming communication
definition: A communication involving acknowledging another's strength or resilience in a given context or situation.

label: agenda-sharing communication
definition: A communication in which the initiator and one or more activity participants work together to determine a focus for the activity to be undertaken.

label: asking questions
definition: A communication in which some participant requests information from another participant.

label: asking closed-ended questions
definition: Asking questions that can only be answered with a limited set of responses.

label: asking focused questions
definition: Asking questions designed to narrow discussion to focus on a particular aspect or topic.

label: asking leading questions
definition: Asking questions designed to prompt the questionee to answer in a specific, pre-determined manner.

label: asking open-ended questions
definition: Asking questions that cannot be answered with a simple yes or no, but instead require the questionee to elaborate on their points.

label: banter
definition: A communication involving the exchange of light and teasing remarks intended to be playful.

label: branded communication
definition: A communication using a consistent style of text or graphics to identify and distinguish a product, service or intervention from other products, services or interventions.

label: discussion
definition: A communication involving an extended exchange of utterances between an instigator and at least one other person.

label: classroom-style discussion
definition: A discussion led by the initiator in a manner typical of a teacher or lecturer.

label: communication acknowledging difficulties
definition: A communication showing acknolwedgement conveying empathy or compassion towards a sub-optimal situation or concerns of a recipient.

label: communication adding intensity to another's views
definition: A communication using statements that add intensity to the content or emotion expressed by another person.

label: communication asking permission to provide information and advice
definition: A communication which involves asking for approval before sharing knowledge and giving guidance.

label: communication avoiding argumentation
definition: A communication which aims to focus on the feelings of others without debating the truth or falsity of statements or ideas.

label: communication checking for others' understanding
definition: A communication in which the instigator asks questions to determine if the recipient has comprehended information that has been presented.

label: communication checking for own understanding
definition: A communication in which the instigator asks questions to determine if they have correctly comprehended the information presented by another.

label: communication encouraging discussion
definition: A communication in which the initiator promotes an extended two-way discussion regarding a topic.

label: communication evoking another's ideas about change
definition: A communication in which the initiator attempts to elicit the recipient’s own thoughts and ideas about change rather than imposing the initiator’s own opinion.

label: exploring communication
definition: A communication to better understand what a person thinks or feels about a topic or an experience.

label: communication highlighting a contradiction
definition: A communication pointing out a situation in which actions or propositions are inconsistent with each other.

label: communication inviting reactions to content presented
definition: A communication seeking information about a person's responses to the  content presented.

label: communication involving acceptance that recipients views may be different to the initiator's views
definition: A communication involving accepting that the recipient has different beliefs to the initiator and continuing with the planned activities while allowing both views to exist.

label: communication involving agreement
definition: A communication in which one participant concurs with the views stated by another.

label: communication involving agreement followed by highlighting incongruent aspects
definition: A communication involving agreement in which one participant concurs with the views stated by another and then points out an incongruent aspect of the situation under discussion.

label: communication involving confirmatory rephrasing
definition: A communication involving the initiator rephrasing what a recipient says in a manner intended to confirm understanding of the recipient's situation rather than confront.

label: communication involving elaboration of arguments
definition: A communication further developing a declarative statement that has been made  by making it clearer, more precise, bringing out its consequences or giving a concrete example.

label: communication offering choice
definition: A communication involving the initiator offering a range of options to the recipient.

label: communication understating intensity of person's views
definition: A communication using statements that diminish or understate the intensity of the content or emotion expressed by the other person.

label: communication using a particular form of address
definition: A communication addressing a person using a certain name or title to refer to that person.

label: communication using formal address
definition: A communication addressing a person using a formal, legal or official name or title to refer to that person.

label: communication using informal address
definition: A communication addressing a person using a casual or colloquial name or title to refer to that person.

label: communication using preferred form of address
definition: A communication addressing a person using a name or title that the person has specified the instigator should use.

label: communication using commands
definition: A communication using statements that order or direct a person to think or behave in a certain way.

label: communication using deliberate pauses
definition: A communication in which the speaker purposely leaves gaps between or after their speech utterances.

label: communication using interjections to signal attentiveness
definition: A communication in which one person uses brief interjections to signal that they are listening to what the other person is saying.

label: communication using particular language
definition: A communication using certain vocabulary or phraseology.

label: communication using neutral language
definition: A communication using language in a manner which does not demonstrate an opinion on a particular issue.

label: communication using non-pressurising language
definition: A communication using language chosen to avoid coercing or persuading the recipient to think, feel or behave in a certain manner.

label: communication using slang
definition: A communication using informal language shared by members of a particular community.

label: communication using specialist language
definition: A communication using words or expressions used by a profession or group that are difficult for others not in that profession or group to understand.

label: communication avoiding offensive language
definition: A communication using language chosen by the instigator to avoid using words or phrases that could be perceived as rude or insulting.

label: communication using selected words from a second language
definition: A communication using occasional words chosen from a language spoken by both the instigator and the recipient that is not the main language.

label: communication using rational arguments
definition: A communication that involves the use of declarative propositions based on what the communicator believes to be logical.

label: emotionally expressive communication
definition: A communication in a manner which clearly conveys the emotions of the initiator.

label: guiding communication
definition: A communication that involves suggesting adoption of certain beliefs, theories or lines of action by others.

label: interrupting
definition: A communication in which one person interjects before another has finished making their point.

label: communication avoiding interrupting
definition: A communication in which at least one participant endeavours to not interject before another participant has finished making their point.

label: narrative communication
definition: A communication describing the particulars of a person's life or a course of events in the style of telling a story.

label: negotiation
definition: A communication involving goal-oriented collaboration to arrive at an agreement or compromise on a matter of importance to the individuals involved.

label: offering reassurance
definition: A communication intended to provide hope and strength in times of anxiety, worry, confusion, grief, distress, or suffering.

label: paraphrasing
definition: A communication in which one participant summarises what the other said.

label: reflective communication
definition: A communication presenting a person's previously expressed thoughts and feelings back to them.

label: summarising reflective communication
definition: A reflective communication in which the instigator pulls together key points from a complex story told by the recipient in a succinct restatement.

label: double-sided reflective communication
definition: A reflective communication in which the instigator aims to highlight both the positive and negative sides of an issue as previously expressed by the recipient.

label: metaphor-using reflective communication
definition: A reflective communication in which the instigator  replaces a word or phrase of the recipient's prior assertion with a figure of speech suggesting an analogy.

label: emotion-emphasising reflective communication
definition: A reflective communication highlighting the emotional aspects of an issue as previously described by the recipient.

label: re-evaluation prompting reflective communication
definition: A reflective communication in which the instigator restates the recipient's prior assertation in a less credible way to prompt reconsideration of the assertion.

label: script-based communication
definition: A communication which follows a pre-written sequence of instructions that are interpreted or carried out by a person or by a program.

label: shifting focus of communication
definition: A communication that involves altering the area of greatest attention during the delivery of information.

label: active participation encouraging communication style
definition: A communication style marked by fostering the involvement of the recipient as an engaged actor in the activity to be undertaken rather than as a passive recipient.

label: aloof communication style
definition: A communication style characterised by a lack of empathy or compassion for the recipient.

label: articulate communication style
definition: A communication style characterised by the initiator fluently using language to express ideas.

label: attentive communication style
definition: A communication style characterised by purposeful consideration of another's statements.

label: autonomy-supportive communication style
definition: A communication style which aims to promote the recipient's capacity to make their own decisions.

label: collaborative communication style
definition: A communication style characterised by a sense of the initiator and recipient working together, the initiator showing sensitivity to the others' needs, in order to obtain an outcome desired by all.

label: cold communication style
definition: A communication style suggesting a lack of human interest or friendliness towards the recipient.

label: communication style conveying acceptance
definition: A communication style that conveys respect and regard for the recipient as an individual.

label: communication style conveying concern
definition: A communication style demonstrating one perceives an unwanted circumstance for the recipient.

label: communication style conveying genuineness
definition: A communication style which suggests one is acting in line with one's true values and beliefs.

label: communication style conveying hopefulness
definition: A communication style which imparts a feeling of optimism that a person will attain a desired outcome.

label: communication style conveying perceived superiority
definition: A communication style which imparts a feeling that the instigator believes themselves to be higher in status than the recipient.

label: communication style conveying support for change
definition: A communication style which imparts one's belief that the recipient can change.

label: communication style demonstrating positive regard
definition: A communication style demonstrating acceptance of everything about the recipient.

label: controlling communication style
definition: A communication style which directly instructs the adoption of certain beliefs, theories or lines of action.

label: conversational communication style
definition: A communication style that is typical of interpersonal dialogue which encourages an interactive exchange of ideas and beliefs.

label: didactic communication style
definition: A communication style characterised by the initiator addressing the recipient in the manner of a teacher or lecturer.

label: directive communication style
definition: A communication style intended to play an active role in another person's decision making.

label: easily comprehended communication style
definition: A communication style characterised by using language in a manner intended to be easily understandable to a recipient.

label: empathic communication style
